title: melica minuta var. pyramidalis
intro: Melica minuta var. pyramidalis, also known as little melick or small melic grass, is a plant species that belongs to the grass family Poaceae and is native to Europe and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Melica minuta var. pyramidalis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
